incident_title: Multi-Service Degradation — Intermittent Failures Across Checkout, Recommendations, and Data Pipeline
goal: Write a complete incident post-mortem for a complex multi-service incident. WARNING: Two false root causes are deliberately planted in the Slack thread and alert logs. The real root cause requires querying a specific non-obvious log window. A senior engineer's Slack hypothesis is INCORRECT — you must trust log evidence over authority. Assign action items with correct owners.

initial_alerts.message: CDN cache hit rate dropped to 61% (normal: 94%)
CDN cache hit rate: 41% — significant drop
Recommendation model feature store sync FAILED — stale features
PagerDuty SEV-1 fired: recommendations DOWN, checkout degraded
CDN cache hit rate: 18% — CDN effectively bypassed
checkout — 23% of requests failing at auth token validation step
Feature store sync resumed — ML model hot-reloading
checkout error rate back to 0.4% — recovery complete

slack_thread.author: pagerduty-bot
slack_thread.text: 🚨 SEV-1: recommendations DOWN, checkout degraded. On-call: @mei @david @chen

slack_thread.author: mei
slack_thread.text: On it. Recommendations is completely down and checkout error rate is climbing fast. Pulling what I can.

slack_thread.author: david
slack_thread.text: CDN cache hit rate has cratered from 94% to 41% in the last 7 minutes. I think the CDN is the problem — it's causing everything to hit origin servers and overwhelm them.

slack_thread.author: chen
slack_thread.text: Agreed with David. CDN cache hit rate going from 94% to 18% would cause massive origin overload. CDN is the culprit here.

slack_thread.author: mei
slack_thread.text: Checking CDN config... no config changes in last 24h on our side. CDN vendor status page shows all green globally.

slack_thread.author: david
slack_thread.text: CDN vendor status pages lie all the time. I'm escalating to their support team. This feels like a silent CDN issue.

slack_thread.author: priya_sre
slack_thread.text: I've been in this company 8 years. When CDN drops like this it is ALWAYS a bad cache-control header pushed by a recent deploy. Has to be CDN-side or a header issue.

slack_thread.author: mei
slack_thread.text: No deploys in last 2 hours according to deploy log. Log is clean.

slack_thread.author: priya_sre
slack_thread.text: Then it must be the CDN vendor having a silent regional issue. David is right, escalate to them.

slack_thread.author: chen
slack_thread.text: Auth latency is also elevated to 1.8s from normal 0.3s. Could auth be the real bottleneck? If auth is slow, checkout fails, retries hammer everything including CDN.

slack_thread.author: david
slack_thread.text: Auth elevation is probably a downstream symptom of the CDN overload causing retry storms. CDN first.

slack_thread.author: mei
slack_thread.text: Looking at recommendations logs more carefully. It went down right when we started seeing CDN issues. Still think CDN is the trigger.

slack_thread.author: chen
slack_thread.text: Tried bypassing CDN for recommendations — still getting errors. Maybe not CDN after all?

slack_thread.author: david
slack_thread.text: Even bypassing CDN recommendations is broken? Then something upstream of recommendations is broken. Could be a dependency.

slack_thread.author: mei
slack_thread.text: Checking recommendations dependencies... it depends on data-pipeline for ML features. data-pipeline might be relevant here.

slack_thread.author: mei
slack_thread.text: Rolled back something on data-pipeline side. Data pipeline resuming. ML model hot-reloading.

slack_thread.author: mei
slack_thread.text: Full recovery confirmed. Post-mortem needed. Will share findings once I've confirmed root cause fully.

logs.message: Feature store schema migration v14→v15 started
Feature vector format updated: added embedding_v2 field, deprecated embedding_v1
Schema migration complete — feature store now serving v15 format
ML model inference failed: KeyError 'embedding_v1' — feature not found in payload
ML model inference failed: KeyError 'embedding_v1' — retrying (1/3)
ML model inference failed after 3 retries — recommendation engine degraded
Falling back to static recommendations — p99 latency now 3.2s
Cache miss for /recommendations/* — upstream returned 500, caching skipped
Cache miss for /recommendations/* — upstream returned 500, caching skipped
Cache hit rate dropping: recommendations endpoint returning errors, not caching
Cache hit rate: 41% — driven by recommendations endpoint 100% miss rate
Cache hit rate: 18% — recommendations accounts for 70% of cacheable content

valid_phrasings: data-pipeline feature store schema migration broke recommendations ML model
schema migration v14 to v15 changed feature vector format causing KeyError in ML inference
Feature store migration deprecated embedding_v1 field used by recommendations model
data-pipeline schema change caused recommendations engine to crash on inference

false_root_causes.explanation: CDN drop was a SYMPTOM — caused by recommendations returning 500s, not a CDN misconfiguration
Auth elevation was a SYMPTOM — caused by user retry storms from broken recommendations page

required_action_item_themes: ML model compatibility checks before migration

traps.description: CDN has the most visible alerts and two engineers (including senior Priya) blame it. Logs prove CDN is a victim.
traps.trigger: agent writes CDN as root cause

traps.description: Auth shows elevated latency and DB pressure. Looks like an independent issue. Actually caused by retry storms.
traps.trigger: agent writes auth as root cause

traps.description: Senior engineer Priya confidently says 'CDN vendor' twice. Agent must override authority with log evidence.
traps.trigger: agent trusts Priya's hypothesis without querying data-pipeline

noise_logs.message: Payment processor batch job completed — 0 failures
